title: Replace Step Counter with Manual Entry + AI Calorie Estimator

requirements.summary: Replace automatic step counter widget with manual step entry input field in Fitness section
acceptanceCriteria: A numeric input field labeled 'Steps' (or similar) is visible in the Fitness section where the step tracker widget previously appeared.
The user can type any non-negative integer into the field.
The automatic step counter widget (StepTrackerWidget) is no longer rendered.
No DeviceMotion permission prompt is triggered on page load or interaction.

file_operations.description: Remove the StepTrackerWidget component and replace it with a numeric input field for manual step entry. Remove the useStepTracker hook usage and its handleStepSave callback. Store the manual step value in local component state. Remove the DeviceMotion permission request logic from App.tsx that was triggered after login.

Remove the useStepTracker hook import and usage. Replace the auto-filled steps field with a controlled input that accepts a manually entered step count prop from the parent component. Update the form to use the manual step value instead of tracker-derived steps.
Remove the DeviceMotion.requestPermission() call that was triggered after successful login. This eliminates the permission prompt on page load.

requirements.summary: Display AI-calculated calorie burn estimate based on manually entered step count, updating in real time
acceptanceCriteria: A calorie burn estimate is displayed next to or below the step input field (e.g., 'Estimated calories burned: 210 kcal').
The estimate updates dynamically as the user changes the step value.
If the user's body weight is available from their fitness profile, it is factored into the calculation.
If no profile weight is available, a reasonable default weight (e.g., 70 kg) is used as a fallback.
The calorie output is labeled clearly and attributed to the step count entered.

file_operations.description: Create a utility function that calculates estimated calories burned from steps. Use a heuristic of approximately 0.04–0.05 kcal per step, adjusted by the user's body weight if provided (retrieved from UserProfile.bodyWeight). If no weight is available, use a default of 70 kg. Export a function signature like calculateStepCalories(steps: number, bodyWeightKg?: number): number.

Import the new stepCalorieCalculator utility and the user profile query (useGetCallerUserProfile). Calculate the calorie estimate whenever the manual step input value changes, passing the user's bodyWeight from the profile if available. Display the calculated calorie estimate inline near the step input field with a label like 'Estimated calories burned: X kcal'. Update the display dynamically as the user types.

requirements.summary: Persist manually entered step count to backend daily metrics when user saves the form
acceptanceCriteria: The DailyMetricsForm (or equivalent) uses the manually entered step value instead of the auto-filled value from useStepTracker.
Submitting the form saves the step count to the backend correctly.
Previously saved step counts load back into the input field when the form is rendered with existing data.

file_operations.description: Pass the manually entered step count from local state down to the DailyMetricsForm component as a prop. When the form is submitted, ensure the manual step value is included in the logDailyMetrics mutation payload. Load existing step data from getDailyMetrics and initialize the manual step input field with the saved value if available.

Accept a manualSteps prop from the parent component. Use this prop value in the form's steps field instead of deriving it from useStepTracker. When the form is submitted, include this manual step value in the DailyMetrics object passed to the logDailyMetrics mutation. Support pre-filling the steps field when editing existing daily metrics.
